prompt:
Our support SLA math is currently three copies of a while-loop that adds 30 minutes and checks getDay(), and all three disagree about what '4 business hours from Friday 16:30' means. I need one calendar module to end the argument: bizhours.ts exporting class BusinessCalendar, plain node, no deps, tests run with node --test.

Everything is naive wall-clock time at minute resolution — timestamps are 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM' strings in and out, no Date-object timezone cleverness, no DST, our ops team lives in one office. Reject malformed or impossible timestamps ('2026-02-30T10:00') with an Error wherever they show up.

new BusinessCalendar({ hours, holidays?, now? }). hours is keyed by 'mon'..'sun' (any other key: Error naming it), each day an array of { open: 'HH:MM', close: 'HH:MM' } shifts — zero-padded, in-range, else Error quoting the bad value. Shifts must be in ascending order, open strictly before close, no overlap — back-to-back (close 13:00, open 13:00) is legal and means continuously open. A day can be missing or [] (closed). A calendar with no open shift anywhere in the week is useless and must be refused at construction. holidays: 'YYYY-MM-DD' strings, must be real dates (Error quoting the offender), closed all day, they override the weekday schedule. now: optional () => timestamp function; deadline() must call it fresh every time so tests can move the clock.

isOpen(ts): open bound inclusive, close bound exclusive — 09:00 is open, 17:00 is not. nextOpen(ts): the earliest instant >= ts that is open; an open instant is its own answer. addBusinessMinutes(ts, m): m must be a non-negative integer (Error otherwise); start the clock at nextOpen(ts) — so m=0 IS nextOpen(ts) — consume m open minutes and return where you land; landing exactly on a close is fine, report the close itself, don't roll to the next morning. Additions must walk over lunch gaps, holidays, weekends, month ends and year ends without breaking a sweat. businessMinutesBetween(a, b): open minutes in [a, b), 0 when a === b, Error when b < a; it must exactly invert addBusinessMinutes. deadline(m): addBusinessMinutes(now(), m).

test_bizhours.ts is the acceptance contract — node --test test_bizhours.ts, leave the file alone.
